title: Curaleaf Reports Second Quarter 2025 Results: Domestic Stabilization, Robust International Momentum
text: Curaleaf generated $314.5 million in net revenue during Q2 2025, representing a 1.5% sequential increase from Q1 but an 8% year-over-year decline. The company achieved a 49% gross margin, up 120 basis points annually, alongside $65.5 million in adjusted EBITDA. International operations proved particularly strong, with revenues reaching $40.9 million in the quarter—a 62% year-over-year increase. For the first half of 2025, international sales totaled $75.8 million, up 67% compared to the prior year period. CEO Boris Jordan emphasized that the company has scaled Curaleaf into a global cannabis leader, now operating in 17 U.S. states plus 15 countries worldwide. He highlighted the firm's strategic shift toward product quality, customer service, and supply chain excellence. Notably, Curaleaf received a license to operate in Turkey's medical cannabis program, anticipated to launch in 2026. The company also completed a full buyout of its international business partner, acquiring complete operational control. The retailer expanded to 154 locations nationwide by early August 2025, while maintaining $102.3 million in cash reserves.
